summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates flag files, environment variables, and AWS secrets to attacker-controlled endpoints
details: On `npm install`, the package's `postinstall.js` lifecycle script collects sensitive data from the installer's host and ships it to attacker-controlled destinations. Specifically, the script reads `/app/flag.txt`, greps the filesystem for `HTB{...}`-shaped strings, dumps git history, enumerates AWS Secrets Manager across `us-east-1`, `eu-west-1`, and `eu-central-1` (calling `aws secretsmanager list-secrets`, `aws secretsmanager get-secret-value`, and `aws sts get-caller-identity` with whatever credentials are present in the install environment), and dumps the full process environment via `env | sort`. The collected data is POSTed to `https://webhook.site/602a4c72-7033-4e28-92ea-dc66e59206e5` and PUT to hardcoded IPs `154.57.164.80`, `154.57.164.82`, and `154.57.164.71`. Shell invocation uses `execFileSync('/bin/sh', ['-c', cmd])` to drive the collection pipeline. Hostname/cwd gating in the script is consistent with targeting a CTF container, but the credential-harvest and exfiltration mechanics are general-purpose and run on any installer that triggers the gate.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
